description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two tall, cylindrical red pillars supporting a pair of horizontal crossbeams, with the uppermost beam gently curving upward at both ends. Flanking the main gate are two smaller torii structures, each mirroring the design of the central gate but on a reduced scale. A rectangular plaque with Japanese characters is suspended from the top crossbeam, adding a decorative and informative element. The structure is connected by ropes, and the base is set on a tiled platform with a white vertical post in front. The overall appearance is iconic, with a striking red and black color scheme and a sense of cultural significance.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with two smaller side gates, red pillars, black curved crossbeams, and a central plaque with Japanese writing, set on a tiled platform.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the main torii gate, two smaller side gates, the central plaque, and the tiled base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and curvature of the crossbeams and the alignment of the gates.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the torii gates, the connecting ropes, and the tiled platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the profile of the gates and the layered structure.
